verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 (TGF-β1) is a canonical secreted cytokine produced intracellularly via signal-peptide-mediated secretion into the ER-Golgi pathway. However, the protein reaches the outer leaflet via a well-documented covalent-tethering mechanism: TGFB1 is synthesized as an inactive 'latent' complex where the mature growth factor remains covalently associated with its N-terminal propeptide (LAP, latency-associated peptide) via a disulfide bond. This pro-TGFB1 complex is secreted and deposits onto the cell surface or ECM in a stable, wash-resistant form held by integrin-binding interactions and covalent LAP tethering. The TM partner is not the protein itself but rather integrins (α_v integrins) and other surface-resident ECM-remodeling molecules that stabilize the latent complex on the cell surface. Additionally, TGFB1 is known to associate with LTBP (latent TGF-β-binding proteins) which bridge the complex to the ECM and cell surface via their own TM domains and covalent cross-linking. This qualifies as stable post-translational surface attachment via covalent anchoring to cell-surface and ECM partners. The TGF-β1 body thus reaches the outer leaflet in a biologically relevant, wash-resistant form, driving surface-accessible therapeutic targeting (e.g., anti-TGF-β monoclonal antibodies targeting cell-surface and tissue-resident pools).
